title: Mass. needs 60K new immigrants annually to sustain workforce, new report finds
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The health care, higher education, and construction industries are most vulnerable to the declining immigration population, the report says.
Activists rally outside the Supreme Court to show solidarity with TPS holders.
The health care sector, which relies heavily on a workforce comprised of many TPS holders, faces challenges amid the pending court case on TPS, a new report revealed.

title: Drake Maye, Patriots coaches have a clear focus on what to fix and improve in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots "Nothing worse for an offense than negative plays.
" Josh McDaniels and Drake Maye aren't resting on their laurels after last year's unexpected Super Bowl run.
Danielle Parhizkaran/The Boston Globe FOXBOROUGH — Drake Maye’s ascension as a legitimate MVP candidate played a key role in the Patriots’ unexpected run to Super Bowl LX last year.

title: Kylian Mbappe, French national team greet fans outside hotel in Boston after arriving for World Cup
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: World Cup France will stay in Boston for the 2026 World Cup — which runs from June 11 to July 19.
Kylian Mbappé and France arrived in Boston on Wednesday night.
Photo by Franck FIFE/AFP via Getty Images Les Bleus have officially landed in Boston.

title: Man convicted of murdering Worcester brothers in ‘case of mistaken identity’
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man killed the two brothers outside of the former Diamond Inn on Grafton Street in Worcester, according to prosecutors.
A man was convicted Tuesday of murdering two Worcester brothers in what prosecutors called “a case of mistaken identity.

”Christopher Burns, 42, was found guilty on two counts of first-degree murder in connection with the deaths of Marcus Thibodeau, 27, and James Johnson, 35, in 2021, the Worcester County District Attorney’s Office said in a press release.

title: Former boyfriend of Worcester woman found dead in car accused of murdering her
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The woman’s former boyfriend has a lengthy criminal history and is currently in state prison for a separate case.
Joanna Brown of Worcester was found dead in her car last November and allegedly murdered by her former boyfriend.
Worcester Police Department A Worcester County grand jury has indicted a man for the murder of a woman who was found dead in her car last November.

title: Police: 2 cars drove onto Marblehead football field, 1 later found in ocean
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News “In a preliminary investigation, Marblehead Police determined that two vehicles were involved, and were able to get a description of both vehicles,” Marblehead police said.
Police are searching for the drivers of two vehicles accused of damaging a Marblehead high school’s football field, then dumping one of the cars into the ocean, police said.
Early Wednesday morning, police were called to Marblehead High School’s football field on Humphrey Avenue, Marblehead police announced in a press release on Wednesday.

title: 8 arrested after protesters disrupt City Council meeting on $4.9 billion budget plan
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News The protesters disrupted a meeting about an amendment package to Mayor Michelle Wu’s $4.
9 billion budget plan.
A protestor is led from the City Council chamber in handcuffs Wednesday after a group disrupted the council's vote on the mayor's budget proposal at City Hall in Boston.

title: Red Sox ace Garrett Crochet says lat strain is ‘a lot worse’ than expected

bullets: Boston Red Sox Crochet is not any closer to returning to the mound after last pitching on April 25.

title: Red Sox offense hits new low, becomes first team since 1901 to record this feat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox Boston's first three hitters in the lineup on Wednesday went 0-for-9 with nine strikeouts against Rays starter Drew Rasmussen.
Photo by Parker S.
Freedman/Getty Images By Conor Ryan June 10, 2026 | 6:30 PM 1 minute to read The Boston Red Sox’ listless lineup made history for all of the wrong reasons on Wednesday afternoon.
